briefing.opening: Good morning/afternoon, everyone. I will now begin the climate briefing for May 9, 2026.

sections.title: 🔴 Global Climate Crisis: Warnings and Intensification
sections.content: News reported today strongly warns of the intensifying global climate crisis. Sea level rise is accelerating [20], and Antarctic sea ice has melted to a record low, prompting warnings from researchers [33, 36]. In Alaska, the risks of climate change due to glacier retreat are also being highlighted [30]. Furthermore, in Argentina and the Andes region, fatal hantavirus infection cases have nearly doubled over the past year, with accelerated spread attributed to climate change [21, 40]. West Africa is suffering from heatwaves [32], and the large-scale fires that occurred in East London during a record 40C heatwave in 2022 clearly demonstrate the scale of wildfire threats caused by climate change [41]. These situations, coupled with warnings that the 'Doomsday Clock' is closer to midnight than ever before, raise serious questions about the future of humanity [42].

sections.title: 🇰🇷 Domestic Climate Adaptation and Carbon Neutrality Efforts
sections.content: Domestically, diverse efforts are underway for climate change adaptation and the transition to a carbon-neutral society. The Ministry of Environment has commenced the establishment of the 4th National Climate Crisis Adaptation Plan, to be applied from 2026 to 2030, with price stability and protection of vulnerable groups as key priorities [5]. The necessity for adaptation is further highlighted as climate change is already altering the retail landscape, with summer diapers, for instance, being released in March [3]. Meanwhile, carbon neutrality practices are spreading through 'Visiting Environmental Classrooms' and special environmental education lectures in Incheon's Dong-gu district [4, 7], while Asan, Chungnam, and Bucheon City are encouraging citizen participation through volunteer relays and business agreements to activate resource circulation [8, 12, 14]. However, the May processing of the Carbon Neutrality Framework Act amendment remains uncertain, with key issues still under discussion [6, 10], indicating that efforts for policy advancement will continue. Gunpo mayoral preliminary candidate Han Dae-hee, for example, promised to establish measures for carbon neutrality implementation and renewable energy expansion during a meeting with environmental groups [9, 11, 13], showing increasing interest in climate agendas within the political sphere.

sections.title: 🌱 Accelerating Renewable Energy Transition Domestically and Internationally
sections.content: The transition to renewable energy is steadily accelerating both domestically and internationally. Korea Electric Power Corporation (KEPCO) has launched a solar inverter council to strengthen domestic renewable energy infrastructure [1], and the Ministry of Agriculture, Food and Rural Affairs is expanding policy support, allowing tenant farmers to install agricultural-type solar panels [18]. Chungcheongbuk-do Province, which has one of the lowest electricity self-sufficiency rates nationwide, is exploring strategies to expand renewable energy supply, continuing regional efforts [15, 17]. Internationally, Europe's coal imports are projected to decrease by up to 20% by 2026, with renewable energy and LNG expected to fill the gap [19]. In Denmark, companies like Meta and Amazon are purchasing solar power, with solar's share in corporate power purchase agreements exceeding 35% [16], demonstrating that renewable energy is becoming mainstream in the global energy market.

sections.title: 🌍 International Climate Action and Research
sections.content: Globally, various forms of action and research are actively being undertaken to raise awareness and address climate change. Students in the Pacific region are raising the voices of future generations by filing a lawsuit with the world's highest court to fight climate change [24]. An artist in Asheville is highlighting climate change, activism, and kindness through their work [23, 29], and a man in Maidstone has composed a climate change song for Sir David Attenborough [28], demonstrating the spread of climate messages in the cultural and artistic fields. In the scientific community, a Utah State University graduate is researching the impact of climate change on Arctic wetlands [25], and new research suggests that biomass carbon removal could help address climate change [34], indicating ongoing efforts to find solutions. Furthermore, the IPCC is inviting governments and experts to review the second draft of its 'Climate Change and Cities' report [39], ensuring continuous international policy discussions and cooperation.

briefing.closing: Today's briefing has highlighted the deepening global climate crisis, alongside diverse domestic and international efforts and increasing awareness to overcome it. Global cooperation and action, based on a scientific understanding of climate change, are more crucial than ever. This concludes today's climate briefing.

articles.description: In record 40C heat on 19 July 2022, 18 homes were lost in village of Wennington – a signal for firefighters to adapt, but UK response remains fragmentedWhen neighbours urged Lynn Sabberton and her partner, Terry, to flee from their home in Wennington one day in 2022, the couple weren’t sure they should bother. A fire was burning in their village, on the eastern edge of London, but Terry thought it was too far away to be a problem. Struggling with a lung disease made worse by the record 40C heat

articles.description: With the war on Iran, Ukraine, AI and climate breakdown increasing the likelihood of a nuclear war, the clock stands closer to midnight than ever before. So who decides how many seconds we have left – and can we buy ourselves more time?The Earth is getting hotter. Conflicts are raging, in the Middle East and Ukraine, each increasing the chance of nuclear war. summary.phenomenon: The Doomsday Clock stands closer to midnight than ever before.
summary.cause: The wars in Iran and Ukraine, the rise of AI, and climate breakdown are increasing the likelihood of nuclear war. Additionally, global warming and ongoing conflicts in the Middle East and Ukraine further escalate this risk.
summary.outlook: The Doomsday Clock indicates that, due to the increased possibility of nuclear war and other threats, humanity's future is more imperiled than ever before.
